perceptual_narrative_logs.visual_narrative_log: (1) The video begins with a POV shot of a steaming hot pot in the foreground, while two young men sit across the table. The man on the left wears a denim jacket and the man on the right wears a black leather jacket; both look at the camera with slightly concerned expressions. (2) Suddenly, the scene cuts to the two men holding up two trays of raw sliced meat each, their eyes wide in a comical 'overwhelmed' expression as stylized text appears. (3) The scene shifts to them actively eating the cooked meat with chopsticks, steam rising from the pot. The man on the right gives a firm thumbs up directly to the camera with a satisfied facial expression.
perceptual_narrative_logs.audio_narrative_log: (1) The audio begins with a male voice speaking Korean in a soft, polite tone, followed immediately by a Chinese translation. (2) A rhythmic, upbeat music track with a strong bass line and 'ding' sound effects kicks in, synchronized with the visual transitions of the meat trays appearing. (3) The music continues as the men make enthusiastic eating sounds and a final exclamation of 'Haochi!' (Delicious) is heard in a high-pitched, excited male voice.
perceptual_narrative_logs.text_narrative_log: (1) Functional Text at top: 'POV 🇰🇷歐巴說台灣火鍋太危險了' (POV Oppa says Taiwan hot pot is too dangerous) - Tone: Hook/Humorous. (2) Dialogue text: '欸 吃不下的話 不用勉強' (Hey, if you can't eat it, don't force it) with Korean translation - Tone: Concerned/Literal. (3) Decorative Text: '梅乾西' (It's okay/Gwaenchana), '(吃了第20盤)' (Ate the 20th plate), '叮叮叮叮' (Ding ding ding ding), '怎麼這麼好吃' (How is it so delicious), '好吃!' (Delicious!) - Tone: Energetic/Variety-show style.

visual_human_presence.countingEvidence: 2 main male creators sitting at the table; POV hands visible in foreground.
visual_human_presence.mainCharacterDescription: Two young men; one has black hair and a denim jacket, the other has black hair and a black leather jacket. Both have a 'K-pop idol' or 'clean-cut' aesthetic.

visual_scene_and_style.backgroundDescribe: A dark-toned restaurant interior with red booth seating and warm, low-key lighting typical of a barbecue or hot pot establishment.
visual_scene_and_style.lightingStyle: Warm indoor lighting with steam reflecting light

narrative_and_purpose.mainPurpose: The video aims to entertain by showcasing two Korean men's enthusiastic and 'dangerous' (addictive) experience eating large quantities of Taiwanese hot pot. It uses the trope of 'overeating because it's too good' to promote Taiwanese food culture in a humorous, fast-paced vlog format.

social_sociology_tags.uniqueSignatures: Usage of 'Korean Oppa' persona and dual-language captions to appeal to both Taiwanese and Korean audiences.
social_sociology_tags.freeDescribe: The video relies heavily on 'Mukbang' tropes—large portions, steaming food, and exaggerated facial expressions—to drive engagement.
